identity.name: Whitney Biennial 2026
identity.address: 99 Gansevoort St, New York, NY 10014, USA
identity.location: 99 Gansevoort St, New York, NY 10014, USA

narrative.summary:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

Gansevoort St in the Meatpacking District — Renzo Piano's glass-and-steel monument to American art.

The 82nd edition of the Whitney Biennial. 56 artists, duos, and collectives. March 8 – August 23, 2026. The longest-running survey of contemporary art in the United States.

blocks.title: Arrival
blocks.body:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

99 Gansevoort St — at the foot of the High Line, overlooking the Hudson.

You enter Renzo Piano's asymmetric concrete-and-glass building and take the elevator to the upper floors. The 2026 Biennial is spread across floors 1, 5, and 6 — plus a billboard on Gansevoort Street visible from outside. This edition, co-curated by Marcela Guerrero and Drew Sawyer, foregrounds mood and texture over definitive statements. It's a show about transition, tenderness, tension, and unexpected coexistence.

blocks.title: The Exhibition
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗘𝗫𝗛𝗜𝗕𝗜𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡

56 artists, duos, and collectives examining forms of relationality — interspecies kinships, familial bonds, geopolitical entanglements, technological affinities, shared mythologies.

Standout artists to watch for (per Artsy's 8 picks):
• Works that evoke humor, unease, and tenderness simultaneously
• Immersive environments rather than traditional gallery hanging
• Photography, sculpture, video, installation, and performance

The Whitney Biennial has always been a barometer of where American art is headed. This edition captures a moment of profound transition — it doesn't offer answers, it offers atmosphere.

⭐ The outdoor terraces with Hudson River views are part of the experience
⭐ Floor 5 tends to be the most immersive — start there

blocks.title: Check
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗖𝗛𝗘𝗖𝗞

💰 Admission: $30 adults, $25 seniors, $21 students. Pay-what-you-wish Fridays 5-10pm.

🌐 whitney.org/exhibitions/2026-biennial
📞 (212) 570-3600

𝗢𝗡 𝗩𝗜𝗘𝗪: March 8 – August 23, 2026

𝗛𝗢𝗨𝗥𝗦
Mon, Wed–Thu, Sat–Sun: 10:30 AM – 6:00 PM
Friday: 10:30 AM – 10:00 PM
Tuesday: Closed

𝗕𝗘𝗦𝗧 𝗧𝗜𝗠𝗘𝗦
• Weekday morning: Fewest crowds
• Friday evening (5-10pm): Pay-what-you-wish, livelier crowd
• Combine with a walk on the High Line — the entrance is right there
• Allow 2 hours minimum

discovery.text: Discovered by Disco via neighbourhood research
